title: Neottia biflora
taxonomy: Neottia biflora is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Neottia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is a type of terrestrial orchid, commonly found in woodland environments. Neottia biflora is native to North America and is known for its unique, two-flowered inflorescence. It is a relatively rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and fragmentation.
uses: Neottia biflora is known to have traditional medicinal uses, particularly in East Asian cultures. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Neottia biflora is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's roots are often used to make herbal teas or infusions. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.

intro: Neottia biflora, also known as the two-flowered grass-pink, is a perennial orchid native to North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wound healing and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neottia biflora include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
